title: Oztures Trading Pty Ltd (trading as Binance Australia Derivatives) — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered Oztures Trading Pty Ltd, trading as Binance Australia Derivatives, to pay a $10 million penalty (orders handed down 27 March 2026) after it admitted misclassifying more than 85% of its Australian client base - 524 retail investors - as wholesale clients between July 2022 and April 2023, exposing them to high-risk crypto derivatives products without the consumer protections required for retail clients. The misclassified clients suffered approximately $8.66 million in trading losses and paid approximately $3.89 million in fees; ASIC separately oversaw approximately $13.1 million in compensation to affected clients in 2023.
scope: Admitted misclassifying over 85% of its Australian client base as wholesale rather than retail clients; Ordered by the Federal Court to pay a $10 million penalty; Misclassified clients suffered a combined $8.66 million in trading losses and paid $3.89 million in fees
